registered: 2026-07-10, before any low-rf arm ran (high-rf verdict already known: FAIL, 31x the floor — see conv_ab_prereg.json + conv-ab-qwen30b/reduction.json)
subject: Same published Qwen3-30B-A3B body at sequence_len 256, max_steps 50 — the killed lo configs verbatim (conv-ab-qwen30b/configs/*_lo.yaml); only host and output paths differ. Host: RunPod RTX 3090 (sm_86), axolotl feature/expert-store @ b22b533, bnb feature/experts-4bit @ e7f4d86 source-built sm_86.
bracket_rationale: same bracket as the primary: whole -> routed -> whole gives the pair its own measured floor at this seq/step count (the 50-step seq-256 noise floor is unknown a priori).
purpose: dose-response for the high-rf FAIL. At seq 256 the per-forward routed union is predicted well below the seq-2048 near-full union, so far more rows carry the deterministic fill. Does the divergence grow with fill mass (1 - rf)?
rf_instrument: per-BLOCK one-shot STAGEDCNT (48 lines on the first routed forward, STAGED_COUNT_LOG=1 on routed_lo only) — fixes the primary positive-control's layer-0-only blind spot. Phase-0 mean-rf prior at eff_tokens 256: ~0.69 (predicted mean staged ~80-95/128); layer 0 likely higher (it read 0.969 at seq 2048).

criteria.primary: final eval loss: PASS iff |routed_lo - mean(whole_lo_a, whole_lo_b)| <= |whole_lo_a - whole_lo_b|.
criteria.expectation: FAIL (given the at-scale high-rf FAIL); the question here is MAGNITUDE, not direction.
criteria.dose_response_readout: gap_lo = |routed_lo - mean(whole_lo)| and its ratio to floor_lo, read against measured mean fill (1 - mean rf). H_FILL (fill-mass-proportional mechanism) predicts gap_lo well above the high-rf 0.062 in train-loss terms; H_DISCRETE (per-step corruption independent of fill mass) predicts a comparable gap; routed_lo WITHIN its floor would localize the defect to the high-rf/long-seq regime (e.g. a packing interaction with the routing interception) and redirect the repro.
criteria.secondary: signed per-step train-loss direction: fraction of steps routed_lo > whole_lo_a, vs whole_lo_b > whole_lo_a (coin-flip null).
criteria.controls: step-0 eval identity across all three arms at print precision (forward fingerprint — routed staging is active during routed_lo's step-0 eval); whole arms emit no routed INFO line and no STAGEDCNT (bleed); STAGEDCNT present in routed_lo only.

cost_box: 3090 at <= $0.60/hr; projected 3-6 h total (the seq-256 eval loop is staging-bound: ~8x the forwards of seq 2048, each staging all 48 layers). ABORT-and-keep-partials if a single arm exceeds 3 h wall.
notes: eval set = alpaca val 5% packed at 256 (2 evals/arm, steps 0 and 50) dominates wall-clock. Configs byte-identical to the committed lo configs except output_dir/wandb_name (+ whole_lo duplicated into whole_lo_a/whole_lo_b).

amendment_both_legs.registered: 2026-07-11 ~04:10Z, after the OLMoE sweep LAUNCHED but before any of its numbers were read.
amendment_both_legs.why: Qwen3-30B cannot train usably on the 3090: container memlock hard-capped at 8 MB (the 14.5 GB expert home cannot pin -> synchronous pageable H2D per stage) + PCIe negotiated gen1. One training step did not finish in 14 min (no-eval diagnostic, peak 16.4 GB — memory was NOT the binding constraint). Owner chose BOTH legs.
amendment_both_legs.leg_1_olmoe_3090: 6 arms on the existing 3090: hi bracket seq 2048 + lo bracket seq 256, each whole_a -> routed -> whole_b, 150 steps (the old 15-step OLMoE probe was too short by construction). Same primary criterion per bracket; dose-response readout = gap-over-floor at measured rf_hi vs rf_lo (per-block STAGEDCNT in both routed arms). OLMoE = allenai/OLMoE-1B-7B-0924, E=64, same body otherwise.
amendment_both_legs.leg_2_qwen30b_bigger_card: 3-arm lo bracket (seq 256, 50 steps, the committed lo configs + the prediction_loss_only deviation) on a PRO-6000-class host AFTER the OLMoE leg completes (one pod at a time). Its high-rf point already exists: the primary FAIL (0.062, 31x floor). Host gate: driver >= 580 AND memlock unlimited/>=16 GB.
amendment_both_legs.interpretation_note: OLMoE hi bracket doubles as an architecture-transfer check of the Qwen3-30B high-rf FAIL: if OLMoE hi ALSO fails at 150 steps, the earlier 15-step OLMoE pass was a horizon artifact; if OLMoE hi passes, the divergence has an architecture-dependent component.

deviations_for_24gb_fit.recorded: 2026-07-11, BEFORE any valid arm produced a number (arm-1 attempt #1 OOMed at eval batch 876/1036 and was discarded).
deviations_for_24gb_fit.why: The primary A/B ran on a 96GB PRO 6000; this repro is on a 24GB 3090. At seq 256 the alpaca val 5% packs into 1036 eval batches (8x the primary's ~130 at seq 2048), and HF Trainer's eval logit path (Qwen3 vocab ~152k) overran 24GB even with eval_accumulation_steps=grad_accum(4). Steady-state weights are ~7GB (memory: model runs on a 12GB A2000 with offload), so the overrun is eval-time, not the model.
deviations_for_24gb_fit.change: prediction_loss_only=True forced into axolotl's causal trainer builder (schema doesn't expose it). eval_loss is computed identically with or without gathering predictions, so the measured quantity and the whole/routed treatment are unchanged; it only stops the unused logit gather. Applied to ALL THREE arms equally, so the bracket floor stays fair.
deviations_for_24gb_fit.metric_neutrality_check: whole_lo_a's final eval under this build should track the primary whole arms' trajectory shape (started 5.3 -> ~1.5 band); if it doesn't, the deviation is NOT neutral and the run is void.
deviations_for_24gb_fit.escalation: if prediction_loss_only did NOT make it fit (i.e. the 23GB was steady-state, not logits), the 3090 is too small for this config -> re-run on a bigger card, do not shrink the val set (that would change the floor's noise).
